feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Sales order not approving for TAS item cat.
entry.content:
The user has created Purchase order for 1 line item without reference to the Purchase requisition no. and that too before approving the sales order. So now, the PR field in the schedule lines tab is showing blank for that line item and they are not able to approve the sales order. Also, the invoice and accounting document has been completed for this order.

Can someone please tell is there any way to approve this sales order because user is not agreeing to make a new order instead?

entry.title: Strategy Group 55: PIRs Consumption with reference to Sales Order Creation.
entry.content:
I'm working with Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs) linked to Characteristic/Configuration Data for Product Variants, and I need your insights to resolve a behavior I’ve encountered.

🎯 Objective:

Create PIRs for a product variant (Strategy Group 55) with assigned characteristics/configuration.

PIRs should only be consumed when a sales order with matching configuration is created.

PIRs should generate planned orders, but not convert to production orders until a matching sales order is received.

Lower-level components can be procured based on PIRs.

🧪 Test Scenario:

MRP View:

Configurable Material (Strategy 25): Individual/Collective = Individual

Product Variant (Strategy 55):

Individual/Collective = Collective

Assigned Configurable Material and completed characteristic assignment.

Maintained Planning Variant with characteristic values.

MD61: Created PIR for Product Variant.

MD04: PIR’s configuration was carried into the planned order, and lower-level components were planned correctly after MRP run.

⚠️ Issue / GAP Identified:
When I create a sales order with a different characteristic combination than the one in the PIR, the PIR is still being consumed - even though the configuration does not match. (other word, to be accurate 95% matches)

📌 Additional Info:

No Planning Table or Planning Profile used (per Strategy 55 behavior).

Assumption: PIR consumption is happening for closest match if no exact match is available.

Has anyone encountered this? Am I missing a key configuration or step to ensure that PIRs are only consumed for 100 % matching configurations?


Would appreciate any guidance or shared experiences!
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Down payment in sales order
entry.content:
Hello SAP team,

I would like to know if it is possible to manage down payment at header level in a sales order?

My client says that the business can have more than 50 lines items in a sales order. To ease their tasks, they are asking if it is possible to manage that at header level.

Please provide me your inputs. Thanks

entry.title: Document Currency is not changing at invoice level
entry.content:
Hi All,

While creating the Invoice through Vf01, for one of the invoice type (YSCI) currency is getting converted to RON from EUR, but there is another invoice type (YSCC) so where the currency is not getting converted .

i trying checking custom code but there is no custom logic is written for this scenario.

i am stuck here Any help will be appreciated, any hint like where is the standard code is written for currency conversion etc. or any configuration.

entry.title: How Loading date is calculated in sale order
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m trying to understand how the Loading Date is calculated in SAP during sales order processing. And the field is available in VBEP table.

Could someone please explain

Best regards,

Bharath

entry.title: User Exits - Adding Condition type in sale order
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am adding condition type (ZIOB) in my sale order(va02). That condition type not coming in invoice. 

My requirement is if i am adding condition type in sale order that will be reflected in invoice also. kindly suggest user exits for my requirement.

entry.title: Bapi for Advanced Return Management order creation
entry.content:
Hello, 

I need to create from a Sales order through Bapi a return order based on the Advanced return order functionality.

Unfortunately actually we found only the BAPI to create Standard Return order but no BAPI for the ARM that can be used also to map the follow Up Activities. Can you please help us to understand if there is some functionality that fits our requirement?

Thanks

entry.title: Require Sending Plant & Receiving Plant details of STO
entry.content:
can a single report/Table define GRN details of a STO along with below details

PO no

Outbound Delivery

Sending Plant

Receiving Plant

GRN Qty

entry.title: Creating Sales Orders with Billing Plans and Delivery Dates via API
entry.content:
While working on an integration to create sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I came across two relevant OData APIs provided by SAP:

  • OData V4: CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • OData V2: API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

I encountered some unexpected limitations and I am hoping you could help me with a best practice approach. 

The Problem

Goal:

  • Create a Sales Order via API

  • Include a billing plan on item level

  • Include the requested delivery date on item level

What I Found

V2 – API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

  • Supports billing plan at item level 

  • Does not support Requested Delivery Date at item level directly in the item payload 

    • Strangely enough, the “Create Sales Order” Fiori app does let you enter requested delivery dates per item manually

    • You can only set it via a second call to the schedule line entity (to_ItemScheduleLine)

V4 – CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • No support for billing plans 

  • Does support Requested Delivery Date per item

The Workaround I’m Using

Since I want both the billing plan and requested delivery date, I’m forced to:

  1. Use V2 API to create the Sales Order with Billing Plan.

  2. Make a second API call to /A_SalesOrderItemScheduleLine to update the RequestedDeliveryDate.

My Questions

  • Why is OData V4, a newer version, missing such key functionality like billing plans?
  • Is there a reason why requested delivery date is not available directly in the item payload in V2?
  • Are there other options or best practices to handle this more efficiently?
  • Is there a roadmap for V2? Or will changes only apply for newer versions?

entry.title: Issue with Returns Pulling Current MAP Instead of Original MAP
entry.content:
We're running into an issue where the MAP (Moving Average Price) on a return is being calculated using the current MAP, rather than the MAP that was in place at the time of the original sales document — even though the return is correctly linked to the original sales invoice.

For some context:
Our business frequently purchases the same product at different prices, which causes the MAP to fluctuate. In this particular case, a customer bought an item when the MAP was £17.33. We then restocked that item at a different price, and the MAP updated accordingly. When the customer later returned the item, the return document used the new MAP of £18 instead of the original £17.33.

Is there a way to configure the system so that returns pull the MAP from the original sales document, rather than using the current MAP at the time of return?

Any help or insight would be much appreciated!

entry.title: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner
entry.content: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner in J7LE. This field is not editable

entry.title: Require Current Date VK11 Pricing
entry.content:
Hello All,

Basically, Price in VK11 is maintained with From Date and To Date. But we need to develop a different auto generated Pricing report which should be based on current date, So I need the below marked Table-Field to write the FS. 

Please guide me to get the data.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: TCS Configuration for Scrap Sales in India (SAP Public Cloud) – Threshold Based Rate Application
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We need guidance on the complete configuration steps for calculating TCS (Tax Collected at Source) on scrap sales in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – India localization.

Our requirement is as follows:

  • 0.1% TCS should apply when the customer crosses the ₹50 lakhs threshold in the financial year.

  • 1% TCS should apply from the first scrap sale if no PAN is available (i.e., no threshold consideration).

  • This TCS should be calculated in the scrap sales order accordingly.

Kindly provide the detailed configuration steps, including:

  • Relevant Tax Code / Condition Type settings

  • Customer master data changes (if any)

  • Threshold control setup

  • Assignment in tax procedure or SSCUI details (if applicable)

This is critical for compliance and we request your urgent help.

Thanks & regards,
Vishal

entry.title: Material Substitution with Reason 0006 - Missing Confirmed Quantities
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I’d like to ask you about the following scenario.
We do material substitution in the sales order using substitution reason 0006 (ProdSel - Order only).

In the material determination records we give multiple alternative materials (to be selected according to availability), of which one has the MRP indicator set so that requirements for unconfirmed order quantities get created for that material.

In the customizing for the scope of ATP check we have the setting that at the end of RLT all order quantities should get confirmed.
However, in this substitution scenario SAP does not consider that setting. Instead, it considers only the ATP quantities up to the requested date and leaves the remaining quantity unconfirmed.

My question is: is there a way to get the confirmed quantity based on RLT for the item with the MRP-relevant material?

Thanks and regards
Helmut

entry.title: Setting of different Credit Limit Days as per different customer
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

Can we set different Credit Limit days for different Customer, so that Business can decide what Maximum Pay Period to be provided to different customer as per their sales/payment record. If the Credit Limit day goes over, the Customer will be automatically Blocked for delivery.

Customer A: 90 days

Customer B: 100 days

Customer C: 110 days.

If this configuration is possible, could you please tell me the process.

entry.title: Manage Customer returns - greyed out refund control
entry.content:
In the app "Manage Customer Returns - Version 2", Once you choose the refund control "Decide later" in order to first execute the Returns delivery and inspection process, you can not go back to modify the refund control option from "Decide later" to any other option like "Create credit memo request / Create credit memo / No Refund".

Is there a reason why it is greyed out and not allowing to be "Decided later" if that makes more sense from a process perspective?

And is there anyway to overrun it and choose a specific refund control like the creating of a credit memo request or a credit memo.

Thank you all in advance for any answers or ideas!

entry.title: SD Sales schedule agreements VA31 automatic creation of delivery dates
entry.content:
How would you organize this process?

Office staff would like to create scheduling agreements.
For example, the same articles should always be delivered automatically on Mondays within a validity period.
Example: Hospital receives new food every Monday.

Variant 1: Create VA31 delivery schedules manually -> but this is a lot of work.
Such a button “Generate delivery schedule automatically until the end of the validity period”

Variant 2: Customer sends via EDI ?
Variant 3: FIORI “Import Sales Contract” with Excel.

Is there another variant?

Thank you!

entry.title: Sales order Creation from purchase order
entry.content:
We are automating the creation of sales order. We will receive a PO via idoc and CPI will convert it into ORDERS05 and with that we will create SO. Currently after all mapping we have created SO, but idoc is still in 51 status with error message 113 items displayed.

 

Can anyone highlight what could be the issue.

entry.title: Calculation technique in SAP SD
entry.content: Hi Gurus, I am looking at a way to replicate this invoice with these calculations in SAP SD as per requirement from client. Please share some advice on how to achieve this.

entry.title: Down Payment at header level of a Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello SAP team,

I have some questions regarding down payment at header of a sales order.

1. Is it possible to enable a billing plan at header of a sales order? If yes, how do i achieve this?

2. How does a down payment at header level of a sales order works?

3. Is it possible to use percentage based and not value based?

Thanks for your inputs
